paper: Chetverikov et al., The Trimmed Iterative Closest Point Algorithm, ICPR 2002 / Image Vis. Comput. 2005
claim: On clean KITTI odometry, FRMSD auto-overlap sticks to the min_overlap floor (0.800); fixed overlap at the default 0.900 is within ~0.02 pt RPE of auto but trades throughput and does not consistently improve whole-run ATE.

protocol.seed: first-pose anchor, --no-gt-seed velocity prediction after frame 0

conclusion: FRMSD auto-overlap on KITTI full sequences sticks to mean overlap 0.800, so the committed auto row behaves like a fixed 80% trim on clean driving data. Switching to fixed overlap 0.900 improves translational RPE by about 0.02 percentage points on seq00/07 but cuts throughput by 7-39% and worsens seq00 whole-run ATE by about 38%. The frozen bundle therefore keeps dense auto-overlap as the main KITTI row.
